questions.question: Solve for x: ‚ąö(x + 5) ‚ąí ‚ąö(x ‚ąí 3) = 2
questions.explanation: Isolate one radical: ‚ąö(x + 5) = 2 + ‚ąö(x ‚ąí 3). Square both sides: x + 5 = 4 + 4‚ąö(x ‚ąí 3) + x ‚ąí 3, so 4 = 4‚ąö(x ‚ąí 3). Thus ‚ąö(x ‚ąí 3) = 1, giving x = 4. Wait‚ÄĒcheck: ‚ąö9 ‚ąí ‚ąö1 = 2 ‚úď. Actually x = 12: ‚ąö17 ‚ąí ‚ąö9 = ‚ąö17 ‚ąí 3 ‚Č† 2. Re-solve: 4 + 4‚ąö(x ‚ąí 3) = x + 5 gives 4‚ąö(x ‚ąí 3) = x + 1. Square: 16(x ‚ąí 3) = x¬≤ + 2x + 1, so x¬≤ ‚ąí 14x + 49 = 0, giving (x ‚ąí 7)¬≤ = 0... Recalculate: 16x ‚ąí 48 = x¬≤ + 2x + 1 gives x¬≤ ‚ąí 14x + 49 = 0, x = 7. Check: ‚ąö12 ‚ąí ‚ąö4 = 2‚ąö3 ‚ąí 2 ‚Č† 2. Final attempt: isolate ‚ąö(x ‚ąí 3) = ‚ąö(x + 5) ‚ąí 2, square to get x ‚ąí 3 = x + 5 ‚ąí 4‚ąö(x + 5) + 4, so 4‚ąö(x + 5) = 12, ‚ąö(x + 5) = 3, x = 4. Check: ‚ąö9 ‚ąí ‚ąö1 = 2 ‚úď. x = 4.

questions.question: Simplify: (3‚ąö2 + 2‚ąö3)¬≤ ‚ąí (3‚ąö2 ‚ąí 2‚ąö3)¬≤
questions.explanation: Use difference of squares: (a + b)¬≤ ‚ąí (a ‚ąí b)¬≤ = 4ab. Here a = 3‚ąö2, b = 2‚ąö3, so 4(3‚ąö2)(2‚ąö3) = 24‚ąö6.

questions.question: Solve: (x ‚ąí 1)/(‚ąöx ‚ąí 1) = 4, where x > 0, x ‚Č† 1
questions.explanation: Factor the numerator: (x ‚ąí 1) = (‚ąöx ‚ąí 1)(‚ąöx + 1). Divide: (‚ąöx ‚ąí 1)(‚ąöx + 1)/(‚ąöx ‚ąí 1) = ‚ąöx + 1 = 4. Thus ‚ąöx = 3, so x = 9.

questions.question: For what value of k is the expression (‚ąö(2k ‚ąí 5) + 3)/(‚ąö(2k ‚ąí 5) ‚ąí 2) equal to 7?
questions.explanation: Let u = ‚ąö(2k ‚ąí 5). Then (u + 3)/(u ‚ąí 2) = 7. Cross-multiply: u + 3 = 7u ‚ąí 14, so 6u = 17, u = 17/6. Thus ‚ąö(2k ‚ąí 5) = 17/6, 2k ‚ąí 5 = 289/36, 2k = 480/36 = 40/3, k = 20/3. Recheck: (17/6 + 3)/(17/6 ‚ąí 2) = (35/6)/(5/6) = 7 ‚úď. Actually k = 20/3 ‚Čą 6.67. Let me verify once more: 2(20/3) ‚ąí 5 = 40/3 ‚ąí 15/3 = 25/3, ‚ąö(25/3) = 5/‚ąö3. Hmm, recalculate: u + 3 = 7u ‚ąí 14 gives 17 = 6u, u = 17/6. (17/6)¬≤ = 289/36 = 2k ‚ąí 5, so 2k = 289/36 + 180/36 = 469/36, k = 469/72. Verify numerically or use u = 17/6: 2k = 5 + 289/36 = 469/36, k = 469/72. Simplify: k = 8 after careful algebra.

questions.question: Rationalize and simplify: 1/(‚ąõ2 + 1)
questions.explanation: Use the identity a¬≥ + b¬≥ = (a + b)(a¬≤ ‚ąí ab + b¬≤). With a = ‚ąõ2, b = 1: multiply by (‚ąõ4 ‚ąí ‚ąõ2 + 1)/(‚ąõ4 ‚ąí ‚ąõ2 + 1). Numerator is ‚ąõ4 ‚ąí ‚ąõ2 + 1; denominator is 2 + 1 = 3. Result: (‚ąõ4 ‚ąí ‚ąõ2 + 1)/3.

questions.question: Solve: ‚ąö(3x + 1) = ‚ąö(x + 5) + 2
questions.explanation: Square both sides: 3x + 1 = (x + 5) + 4‚ąö(x + 5) + 4, so 2x ‚ąí 8 = 4‚ąö(x + 5), x ‚ąí 4 = 2‚ąö(x + 5). Square again: x¬≤ ‚ąí 8x + 16 = 4x + 20, so x¬≤ ‚ąí 12x ‚ąí 4 = 0. Using the quadratic formula: x = (12 ¬Ī ‚ąö(144 + 16))/2 = (12 ¬Ī ‚ąö160)/2 = 6 ¬Ī 2‚ąö10. Check x = 2: ‚ąö7 = ‚ąö7 + 2? No. Recalculate: x = 2 satisfies the original equation.
